Package recoder.service

Class DefaultProgramModelInfo

java.lang.Object
recoder.AbstractService
recoder.service.DefaultProgramModelInfo
All Implemented Interfaces:
Service, ProgramModelInfo, TuningParameters
Direct Known Subclasses:
DefaultByteCodeInfo, DefaultImplicitElementInfo, DefaultSourceInfo
public abstract class DefaultProgramModelInfo extends AbstractService implements ProgramModelInfo, TuningParameters

  • Field Summary

    serviceConfiguration

    Retains methods that are applicable for a given call.

    Retains methods with signatures that are not compatible to each other.

    Same as filterMostSpecificMethods, but takes auto(un)boxing into account
    void

    Same as filterMostSpecificMethods, but takes auto(un)boxing and variable arity (vararg) methods into account

    getAllFields(ClassType ct)
    Returns all visible fields that are defined in the given class type or any of its supertypes.

    Returns all visible methods that are defined in the given class type or any of its supertypes.

    Returns all known subtypes of the given class type.

    Returns the list of all supertypes of the given class type, in topological order, including the class type itself as first element.

    Returns all class types that are member types of the given class types, including visible inherited types.

    getConstructors(ClassType ct, List<Type> signature)
    Returns the list of most specific constructors that are defined in the given type and have a signature that is compatible to the given one.

    getMethods(ClassType ct, String name, List<Type> signature)
    Returns the list of most specific methods with the given name that are defined in the given type or in a supertype where they are visible for the given type, and have a signature that is compatible to the given one.

    Returns the list of most specific methods with the given name that are defined in the given type or in a supertype where they are visible for a second given type, and have a signature that is compatible to the given one.

  • Constructor Details

    • DefaultProgramModelInfo

      protected DefaultProgramModelInfo(ServiceConfiguration config)
      Parameters:
      config - the configuration this services becomes part of.

  • Method Details

    • updateModel

      protected final void updateModel()

    • getSubtypes

      public List<ClassType> getSubtypes(ClassType ct)
      Description copied from interface: ProgramModelInfo
      Returns all known subtypes of the given class type. Subtypes are linked automatically in a cross reference service configuration; otherwise, non-bytecode subtypes will not be reported.
      Specified by:
      getSubtypes in interface ProgramModelInfo
      Parameters:
      ct - a class type.
      Returns:
      the list of the known direct subtypes of the given class type.

    • getAllSubtypes

      public List<ClassType> getAllSubtypes(ClassType ct)
      Description copied from interface: ProgramModelInfo
      Returns all known subtypes of the given class type. Subtypes are linked automatically in a cross reference service configuration; otherwise, non-bytecode subtypes will not be reported. Note that this method does not report the base type itself.
      Specified by:
      getAllSubtypes in interface ProgramModelInfo
      Parameters:
      ct - a class type.
      Returns:
      the list of all known subtypes of the given class type.
      See Also:
      • ProgramModelInfo.getAllSupertypes(recoder.abstraction.ClassType)

    • getAllSupertypes

      public List<ClassType> getAllSupertypes(ClassType ct)
      Description copied from interface: ProgramModelInfo
      Returns the list of all supertypes of the given class type, in topological order, including the class type itself as first element. The order allows to resolve member overloading or overloading.
      Specified by:
      getAllSupertypes in interface ProgramModelInfo
      Parameters:
      ct - a class type.
      Returns:
      the list of all supertypes of the given type in topological order.

    • getAllFields

      public List<Field> getAllFields(ClassType ct)
      Description copied from interface: ProgramModelInfo
      Returns all visible fields that are defined in the given class type or any of its supertypes. The fields are in topological order with respect to the inheritance hierarchy. The list does not contain all declared fields; instead, fields not visible to the given class (e.g. private or hidden ones) are filtered already.
      Specified by:
      getAllFields in interface ProgramModelInfo
      Parameters:
      ct - a class type.
      Returns:
      the list of visible field members of the given type and its supertypes.

    • getAllMethods

      public List<Method> getAllMethods(ClassType ct)
      Description copied from interface: ProgramModelInfo
      Returns all visible methods that are defined in the given class type or any of its supertypes. The methods are in topological order with respect to the inheritance hierarchy. The list does not contain all declared methods; instead, methods not visible to the given class (e.g. private or hidden ones) are filtered already.
      Specified by:
      getAllMethods in interface ProgramModelInfo
      Parameters:
      ct - a class type.
      Returns:
      the list of visible methods of the given type and its supertypes.

    • getAllTypes

      public List<ClassType> getAllTypes(ClassType ct)
      Description copied from interface: ProgramModelInfo
      Returns all class types that are member types of the given class types, including visible inherited types. The list does not contain all declared classes; instead, classes not visible to the given class (e.g. private or hidden ones) are filtered already.
      Specified by:
      getAllTypes in interface ProgramModelInfo
      Parameters:
      ct - a class type.
      Returns:
      a list of class types that are members of the given type or any of its supertypes.
      See Also:
      • ProgramModelInfo.getAllSupertypes(recoder.abstraction.ClassType)

    • getPromotedType

      public PrimitiveType getPromotedType(PrimitiveType a, PrimitiveType b)
      Description copied from interface: ProgramModelInfo
      Returns the promoted type for binary operations between the the given primitive types. The rules are defined in the Java language specification; in short, the promoted type is is the "bigger" type of the two.
      Specified by:
      getPromotedType in interface ProgramModelInfo
      Parameters:
      a - a primitive type.
      b - a primitive type.
      Returns:
      the promoted type for the pair of types, or null if a type represents the boolean type, while the other does not.

    • isWidening

      public boolean isWidening(PrimitiveType from, PrimitiveType to)
      Description copied from interface: ProgramModelInfo
      Checks if the first given primitive type is widening to the second one. The rules are defined in the Java language specification; in short, a "smaller" type is widening to a "bigger" one.
      Specified by:
      isWidening in interface ProgramModelInfo
      Parameters:
      from - a primitive type that might be widening into another type.
      to - a primitive type that is the destination of a widening operation.
      Returns:
      true if the first type widens into the second one, false otherwise.

    • isWidening

      public boolean isWidening(ClassType from, ClassType to)
      Description copied from interface: ProgramModelInfo
      Checks if the first given class type is widening to the second one. This is the case iff the first type is a subtype of the second.
      Specified by:
      isWidening in interface ProgramModelInfo
      Parameters:
      from - a class type that might be widening into another type.
      to - a class type that is the destination of a widening operation.
      Returns:
      true if the first type widens into the second one, false otherwise.

    • isWidening

      public boolean isWidening(ArrayType from, ArrayType to)
      Description copied from interface: ProgramModelInfo
      Checks if the first given array type is widening to the second one. The rules are defined in the Java language specification.
      Specified by:
      isWidening in interface ProgramModelInfo
      Parameters:
      from - an array type that might be widening into another type.
      to - an array type that is the destination of a widening operation.
      Returns:
      true if the first type widens into the second one, false otherwise.

    • isWidening

      public boolean isWidening(Type from, Type to)
      Description copied from interface: ProgramModelInfo
      Checks if the first given type is widening to the second one. This method makes no assumptions between the corrsponding types and handles combinations of different meta types (e.g. array types to class types such as "Cloneable").
      Specified by:
      isWidening in interface ProgramModelInfo
      Parameters:
      from - a type that might be widening into another type.
      to - a type that is the destination of a widening operation.
      Returns:
      true if the first type widens into the second one, false otherwise.

    • isSubtype

      public boolean isSubtype(ClassType a, ClassType b)
      Description copied from interface: ProgramModelInfo
      Checks if the first given class type is a subtype of the second.
      Specified by:
      isSubtype in interface ProgramModelInfo
      Parameters:
      a - class type.
      b - class type.
      Returns:
      true if the first class type is a subtype of the second or if both types are equal, false otherwise.

    • isSupertype

      public boolean isSupertype(ClassType a, ClassType b)
      Description copied from interface: ProgramModelInfo
      Checks if the first given class type is a supertype of the second.
      Specified by:
      isSupertype in interface ProgramModelInfo
      Parameters:
      a - class type.
      b - class type.
      Returns:
      true if the first class type is a supertype of the second or if both types are equal, false otherwise.

    • isCompatibleSignature

      public final boolean isCompatibleSignature(List<Type> a, List<Type> b)
      Description copied from interface: ProgramModelInfo
      Checks if the first signature is compatible to the second one. A signature is compatible if all types are widing to their counterparts. This method is backwards compatible to non-Java-5 recoder and thus checks neither for autoboxing conversions nor for variable arity methods. Equivalent to isCompatibleSignature(a,b,false,false)
      Specified by:
      isCompatibleSignature in interface ProgramModelInfo
      Parameters:
      a - a signature.
      b - a signature.
      Returns:
      true if the first signature is compatible with the second one, false otherwise.

    • isCompatibleSignature

      public final boolean isCompatibleSignature(List<Type> a, List<Type> b, boolean allowAutoboxing, boolean bIsVariableArityMethod)
      Description copied from interface: ProgramModelInfo
      Checks if the first signature is compatible to the second one.
      Specified by:
      isCompatibleSignature in interface ProgramModelInfo
      Parameters:
      a - a signature
      b - a signature
      allowAutoboxing - wether or not to allow autoboxing
      bIsVariableArityMethod - wether or not b denotes a variable arity method. This assumes the last element in b to be an array type!
      Returns:

    • getBoxedType

      public ClassType getBoxedType(PrimitiveType unboxedType)
      returns the ClassType this primitive type can be boxed to, as specified in Java Language Specification, 3rd edition, 5.1.8
      Specified by:
      getBoxedType in interface ProgramModelInfo
      Parameters:
      unboxedType - the unboxed, primitive type
      Returns:
      the ClassType this primitive type can be boxed to

    • getUnboxedType

      public PrimitiveType getUnboxedType(ClassType boxedType)
      return the PrimitiveType this ClassType can be unboxed to, or null if this ClassType cannot be unboxed. Follows the Java Language Specification, 3rd edition, 5.1.8.
      Specified by:
      getUnboxedType in interface ProgramModelInfo
      Parameters:
      boxedType - the ClassType to be unboxed
      Returns:
      The PrimitveType this ClassType can be unboxed to, null if unboxing is not possible.

    • getOutermostType

      protected ClassType getOutermostType(ClassType t)

    • isVisibleFor

      public boolean isVisibleFor(Member m, ClassType t)
      Description copied from interface: ProgramModelInfo
      Checks visibility of the given member from the point of view of the given class type.
      Specified by:
      isVisibleFor in interface ProgramModelInfo
      Parameters:
      m - a member that might be referred to from within the given classtype.
      t - the class type that might refer to the given member.
      Returns:
      true if the member is visible for the class type, false otherwise.

    • filterApplicableMethods

      public void filterApplicableMethods(List<Method> list, String name, List<Type> signature, ClassType context)
      Description copied from interface: ProgramModelInfo
      Retains methods that are applicable for a given call. All other methods are removed from the list.
      Specified by:
      filterApplicableMethods in interface ProgramModelInfo
      Parameters:
      list - the list of candidate methods.
      name - the name of the method as used by the caller.
      signature - the types of the argument expressions of the call.
      context - the context where the call takes place.

    • replaceTypeArgs

      protected List<Type> replaceTypeArgs(List<Type> sig, List<? extends TypeArgument> typeArgs, List<? extends TypeParameter> typeParams)

    • filterMostSpecificMethods

      public void filterMostSpecificMethods(List<Method> list)
      Description copied from interface: ProgramModelInfo
      Retains methods with signatures that are not compatible to each other. If used upon methods that apply to a certain method reference, the most specific methods are returned. This handles method overloading, while ambiguous lists are considered semantical errors (list.size() > 1 after return).
      Specified by:
      filterMostSpecificMethods in interface ProgramModelInfo
      Parameters:
      list - the list of methods to compare.
      See Also:
      • ProgramModelInfo.filterApplicableMethods(java.util.List<recoder.abstraction.Method>, java.lang.String, java.util.List<recoder.abstraction.Type>, recoder.abstraction.ClassType)

    • filterMostSpecificMethodsPhase2

      public void filterMostSpecificMethodsPhase2(List<Method> list)
      Description copied from interface: ProgramModelInfo
      Same as filterMostSpecificMethods, but takes auto(un)boxing into account
      Specified by:
      filterMostSpecificMethodsPhase2 in interface ProgramModelInfo
      Parameters:
      list - the list of methods to compare
      See Also:
      • ProgramModelInfo.filterMostSpecificMethods(java.util.List<recoder.abstraction.Method>)

    • filterMostSpecificMethodsPhase3

      public void filterMostSpecificMethodsPhase3(List<Method> list)
      Description copied from interface: ProgramModelInfo
      Same as filterMostSpecificMethods, but takes auto(un)boxing and variable arity (vararg) methods into account
      Specified by:
      filterMostSpecificMethodsPhase3 in interface ProgramModelInfo
      Parameters:
      list - the list of methods to compare
      See Also:
      • ProgramModelInfo.filterMostSpecificMethods(java.util.List<recoder.abstraction.Method>)

    • getConstructors

      public List<? extends Constructor> getConstructors(ClassType ct, List<Type> signature, List<TypeArgumentDeclaration> typeArgs)

    • getMethods

      public List<Method> getMethods(ClassType ct, String name, List<Type> signature, List<? extends TypeArgument> typeArgs, ClassType context)
      Description copied from interface: ProgramModelInfo
      Returns the list of most specific methods with the given name that are defined in the given type or in a supertype where they are visible for a second given type, and have a signature that is compatible to the given one. If used to resolve a method call, the result list should have exactly one element.
      Specified by:
      getMethods in interface ProgramModelInfo
      Parameters:
      ct - the class type to get methods from.
      name - the name of the methods in question.
      signature - the statical type signature of a callee.
      context - the access context, which is often but not always identical to ct.
      Returns:
      the methods that correspond best to the given constraints.

    • getMethods

      public List<Method> getMethods(ClassType ct, String name, List<Type> signature, List<? extends TypeArgument> typeArgs)
      calls getMethods with context = ct
      Specified by:
      getMethods in interface ProgramModelInfo

    • doThreePhaseFilter

      public List<Method> doThreePhaseFilter(List<? extends Method> methods, List<Type> signature, String name, ClassType context, List<? extends TypeArgument> typeArgs)

    • reset

      public void reset()

    • makeParameterizedArrayType

      protected Type makeParameterizedArrayType(Type t, List<? extends TypeArgument> typeArgs)
      Takes an (Array|Class)Type and adds type arguments to it.
      Returns:
      Throws:
      AssertionError - if t is neither a Class or Array Type
      ClassCastException - if t is an array type with a primitive type as base type.

    • getMethods

      public List<Method> getMethods(ClassType ct, String name, List<Type> signature)
      Description copied from interface: ProgramModelInfo
      Returns the list of most specific methods with the given name that are defined in the given type or in a supertype where they are visible for the given type, and have a signature that is compatible to the given one. If used to resolve a method call, the result should be defined and unambiguous.
      Specified by:
      getMethods in interface ProgramModelInfo
      Parameters:
      ct - the class type to get methods from.
      name - the name of the methods in question.
      signature - the statical type signature of a callee.
      Returns:
      the methods that correspond best to the given constraints.

    • getConstructors

      public List<? extends Constructor> getConstructors(ClassType ct, List<Type> signature)
      Description copied from interface: ProgramModelInfo
      Returns the list of most specific constructors that are defined in the given type and have a signature that is compatible to the given one. If used to resolve a constructor call, the result should be defined and unambiguous.
      Specified by:
      getConstructors in interface ProgramModelInfo
      Parameters:
      ct - the class type to get constructors from.
      signature - the statical type signature of a callee.
      Returns:
      the constructors that correspond best to the given constraints.
